title: Chelidonium hylomeconoides

Chelidonium majus, commonly known as Celandine or Greater Celandine, is a perennial plant belonging to the Papaveraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral activities, as well as its potential to treat various conditions such as cancer and skin disorders. The bioactive constituents of Chelidonium majus include alkaloids such as chelidonine, chelerythrine, and berberine, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. Chelidonium majus is commonly used in herbal preparations, including tinctures, infusions, and capsules, as well as being applied topically in the form of creams and salves.
